title: Turkey & Egg Breakfast Wrap (33g Protein, Portable) | Eati
introduction: A turkey and egg breakfast wrap delivers 33g of protein in 375 calories — a portable, hand-held breakfast that beats any drive-through option. Lean turkey, scrambled eggs, light cheese, and spinach rolled in a low-carb tortilla take 8 minutes to make and 3 days to hold in the fridge. The ideal prep-ahead breakfast for weekday mornings.

sections.heading: Ingredients (1 wrap)
sections.content:
- 1 low-carb tortilla (10 inch, ~100 cal)
- 2 oz (60g) sliced deli turkey breast (lean, low-sodium)
- 2 large eggs, scrambled
- 1 oz (28g) reduced-fat cheese (cheddar, Swiss, or mozzarella)
- 1 cup (30g) fresh spinach
- 1 tbsp salsa or hot sauce
- Olive oil spray
- Salt, pepper, optional paprika

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
2. Mist a nonstick skillet with oil and scramble eggs over medium-low heat, 2–3 minutes.
3. Warm tortilla 15 seconds in microwave or 30 seconds in dry pan.
4. Layer tortilla: turkey, eggs, cheese, spinach, salsa.
5. Fold sides in, then roll tightly from the bottom.
6. Optional: sear seam-side down in dry pan 1 min for a crisp finish.
7. Slice in half, log your serving in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 375 kcal per wrap (base recipe)
Fat: 13g
Protein: 33g
Carbs: 22g (10g fiber from low-carb tortilla)

Use 3 egg whites + 1 whole egg to drop to 320 cal with 31g protein. Standard tortilla instead of low-carb: +80 cal.

sections.heading: Why Breakfast Wraps Beat Drive-Thru Every Time
sections.content:
Breakfast wraps are the ultimate weekday breakfast because they're portable, filling, and macro-friendly. Here's how this homemade version stacks up:

Comparison to popular options:
• McDonald's Bacon Egg Cheese Biscuit: 460 cal, 17g protein, 26g fat.
• Starbucks Turkey Bacon Egg White Wrap: 230 cal, 18g protein (but under-fills most people).
• Chick-fil-A Hash Brown Scramble Burrito: 690 cal, 33g protein.
• Taco Bell Breakfast Crunchwrap: 650 cal, 21g protein.
• This recipe: 375 cal, 33g protein.

You get the same protein as Chick-fil-A at half the calories.

Macro breakdown:
• 375 calories
• 33g protein (high)
• 13g fat (4g saturated)
• 22g carbs, 10g fiber (from low-carb tortilla)
• ~8.5g protein per 100 calories

Why the ingredient stack works:
• Turkey breast: leanest deli meat (25g protein per 100g, 2g fat).
• 2 whole eggs: complete protein, choline, vitamin D.
• Reduced-fat cheese: keeps creaminess without calorie bomb.
• Spinach: volume + iron + folate with minimal calories.
• Low-carb tortilla: cuts carbs 60% vs standard flour tortilla.

Satiety:
Protein (33g) + fiber (10g) + some fat (13g) is the satiety trifecta. This wrap keeps most people full until lunch.

For more make-ahead options, see [20 high-protein low-calorie breakfast ideas](/blog/20-high-protein-low-calorie-breakfast-ideas) and [egg muffins meal prep](/blog/egg-muffins-meal-prep-recipe).

sections.heading: Choosing the Best Tortilla and Deli Turkey
sections.content:
These two ingredients drive most of the macro profile. Choose well:

Best tortilla options:

1. Low-carb/high-fiber tortillas:
• Mission Carb Balance: 100 cal, 11g fiber, 9g protein.
• La Tortilla Factory Low-Carb: 80 cal, 12g fiber, 8g protein.
• Tumaro's Low-Carb: 90 cal, 12g fiber, 7g protein.
• Best for weight loss — cuts carbs, adds protein.

2. Whole-grain tortillas:
• 140–180 cal, 4–5g fiber, 4g protein.
• More traditional taste but higher calorie.
• Good for maintenance or if low-carb wraps don't agree with your stomach.

3. Egg wrap (carb-free alternative):
• 80 cal per wrap, 8g protein, 0 carbs.
• Wraps Snap! or Crepini brands.
• Ideal for strict low-carb or keto approaches.

Avoid:
• Standard flour tortillas (180–200 cal, 4g protein).
• 'Spinach' or 'tomato' tortillas — usually same calories with cosmetic flavoring.

Best deli turkey:

Lean turkey breast (ideal):
• 60 cal per 2 oz, 13g protein, 1g fat.
• Boar's Head Ovengold, Applegate Oven-Roasted, Costco Rotisserie.
• Look for: <400mg sodium per 2 oz, no sugar added, 95%+ meat.

Avoid:
• 'Lunch meat' with corn syrup or phosphates (ingredient list tells the truth).
• Honey or glazed turkey — adds sugar without meaningful benefit.
• Turkey bologna or loaf — processed forms with added fat.

Alternative protein options:
• Lean ham: 70 cal, 12g protein per 2 oz (similar to turkey).
• Chicken breast slices: 50 cal, 11g protein per 2 oz.
• Rotisserie chicken (homemade): best flavor, lowest cost.
• Turkey bacon (2 slices): +60 cal, +6g protein. Adds smoky flavor.

For more lean protein strategies, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods).

sections.heading: Meal Prep: 5 Wraps in 15 Minutes
sections.content:
This wrap is made for batch cooking. Here's the weekday strategy:

Sunday prep (15 minutes for 5 wraps):
1. Scramble 10 eggs with salt and pepper (5 minutes).
2. Lay out 5 tortillas.
3. Layer each: 2 oz turkey, 2 eggs' worth of scramble, 1 oz cheese, spinach, salsa.
4. Roll tightly from the bottom, fold sides in.
5. Wrap each individually in foil.
6. Refrigerate up to 4 days, or freeze up to 2 months.

Reheating options:
• Microwave (fastest): Unwrap foil, wrap in damp paper towel, microwave 60–90 seconds.
• Oven: 350°F for 10 min (keeps tortilla crisper).
• Air fryer: 350°F for 6 min, flip halfway (best texture).
• Pan-sear: Slice in half, pan-sear cut-side down 2 min each side.

From frozen:
• Microwave: 2 minutes, flip halfway through.
• Thaw in fridge overnight for best texture, then reheat normally.

Storage tips:
• Refrigerator: 4 days in foil.
• Freezer: 2 months wrapped in foil + Ziploc.
• Never refrigerate sliced wraps — the filling dries out.
• Label with date + initial.

Budget breakdown:
• 1 pack low-carb tortillas (8 ct): $5 = $0.63/wrap.
• 1 lb deli turkey: $8 = $1/wrap.
• 10 eggs: $3 = $0.60/wrap.
• Cheese: $0.80/wrap.
• Spinach: $0.40/wrap.
• Total: ~$3.40 per wrap.

Compared to $7 breakfast sandwiches at coffee shops — and you control the macros.

Portable pairings:
• +60 cal: 1 cup coffee with splash of milk.
• +35 cal: 1/2 cup berries.
• +100 cal: medium apple.

For more portable breakfasts, see [egg muffins meal prep recipe](/blog/egg-muffins-meal-prep-recipe) and [peanut butter protein toast](/blog/peanut-butter-protein-toast-recipe).

sections.heading: 7 Breakfast Wrap Variations
sections.content:
Rotate through these to keep the format interesting:

1. Classic turkey bacon (365 cal, 31g protein):
• Swap deli turkey for 3 slices turkey bacon. Smokier flavor.

2. Southwest (400 cal, 33g protein):
• Base + 1/4 cup black beans + diced bell pepper + cotija + cilantro.

3. Mediterranean (385 cal, 33g protein):
• Base + 2 tbsp hummus instead of cheese + diced cucumber + feta + olives.

4. Chicken ranch (395 cal, 36g protein):
• Replace turkey with 2 oz rotisserie chicken + light ranch + tomato.

5. Spinach & feta (365 cal, 31g protein):
• Skip turkey, double eggs to 3 whole + 2 whites + feta + sundried tomato. Vegetarian option.

6. Buffalo chicken (410 cal, 38g protein):
• Replace turkey with shredded buffalo chicken + blue cheese + celery. Inspired by [one pan cheesy jalapeno chicken](/blog/one-pan-cheesy-jalapeno-chicken-recipe).

7. Avocado + turkey bacon (440 cal, 34g protein):
• Base + 1/4 sliced avocado + 2 slices turkey bacon. Like our [avocado toast with boiled eggs](/blog/avocado-toast-boiled-eggs-recipe) in wrap form.

Lower-calorie modifications:
• Use 3 egg whites + 1 whole egg: saves 55 cal.
• Skip cheese: saves 80 cal.
• Use egg wrap instead of tortilla: saves 20 cal, cuts 15g carbs.
• Minimum calorie version: 265 cal, 31g protein (egg whites + egg wrap + turkey + spinach + salsa).

Kids' version:
• Use whole eggs, regular tortilla, full-fat cheese.
• Add diced avocado and tomato.
• Skip salsa/hot sauce, use a mild dipping sauce.

midArticleCta: 33g of protein in a wrap you can eat at your desk. Log this turkey-and-egg wrap in Eati and start the day on target.
conclusion: A 375-calorie hand-held breakfast with 33g of protein and 10g of fiber. Prep 5 wraps on Sunday, wrap each in foil, and you have a 90-second microwave breakfast better than anything you can buy at a drive-through.
metaTitle: Turkey & Egg Breakfast Wrap: 33g Protein, 375 Calories (Meal Prep Friendly)
metaDescription: Portable turkey and egg breakfast wrap — 33g protein in 375 calories. 15-minute meal prep for 5 wraps, 7 variations, and the best tortilla + turkey buying guide.

faqs.question: How many calories in a turkey and egg breakfast wrap?
faqs.answer: About 375 calories with a low-carb tortilla, 2 oz deli turkey, 2 scrambled eggs, 1 oz reduced-fat cheese, and spinach. Delivers 33g of protein and 10g of fiber. Using egg whites instead of whole eggs drops it to 320 cal.

faqs.question: Can I meal prep breakfast wraps?
faqs.answer: Yes — they're one of the best freezer-friendly breakfasts. Wrap each one individually in foil, refrigerate up to 4 days, or freeze up to 2 months. Reheat 90 seconds in the microwave (wrapped in a damp paper towel) or 6 minutes in the air fryer for better texture.

faqs.question: What's the best tortilla for a high-protein wrap?
faqs.answer: Low-carb high-fiber tortillas like Mission Carb Balance (100 cal, 11g fiber, 9g protein) or La Tortilla Factory give the best macro profile. Egg wraps (Crepini, Wraps Snap!) are great for strict low-carb diets at 80 cal and 8g protein per wrap.

faqs.question: Are breakfast wraps healthy for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes, when built around lean protein and a high-fiber tortilla. The key is controlling the fat-heavy add-ons (cheese, bacon, sauces). This version's 375 cal with 33g protein easily fits a 1,500–1,800 cal fat-loss day and keeps you full until lunch.

faqs.question: How long do breakfast wraps last in the fridge?
faqs.answer: Tightly wrapped in foil, breakfast wraps keep 3–4 days in the refrigerator and 2 months in the freezer. Don't slice in advance — the filling dries out. Keep them whole until you're ready to eat.
